paper.title_en: Energy Flux as an Entanglement Current in Moving-Mirror Radiation
paper.abstract_en: In this work, we investigate the quantum entanglement properties of analog Hawking radiation produced by a moving mirror. Using two detector modes defined through window functions on a quantum field, we quantify the bipartite entanglement established between these modes. Our results reveal that the amount of entanglement accessible to the detectors increases when the mirror follows trajectories with non-monotonic, time-dependent acceleration, which are accompanied by the emission of negative energy flux. This indicates that the negative energy flux acts as a channel through which information can be returned. To substantiate this perspective, we examine how the recovery or reconstruction of the associated partner modes is related to the negative energy flux emitted by the mirror.

decision.selection_reason_zh: 论文研究运动镜模拟霍金辐射中的纠缠特性，聚焦负能量通量作为纠缠通道的角色，属于黑洞热力学领域的基础理论研究。
decision.title_zh: 运动镜辐射中的能量通量作为纠缠流
decision.abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们研究了运动镜产生的模拟霍金辐射的量子纠缠性质。通过使用量子场上由窗口函数定义的两个探测器模式，我们量化了这些模式之间建立的双粒子纠缠。我们的结果表明，当镜子遵循具有非单调、随时间变化的加速度的轨迹时，探测器可访问的纠缠量增加，这些轨迹伴随着负能量通量的发射。这表明负能量通量充当了一个信息可以返回的通道。为了证实这一观点，我们检查了相关伙伴模式的恢复或重构如何与镜子发射的负能量通量相关。
